title: Async functions should not contain synchronous OS calls
description: This rule checks if an async function contains synchronous OS calls, which can lead to performance issues and potential deadlocks.
message: Async function contains synchronous OS call.
recommendation: Use async OS calls or move synchronous calls outside of async functions.

noncompliant.code:
async def my_func():
    import os
    os.system('echo hello')
noncompliant.description: Async function with synchronous os.system call.

compliant.code:
import asyncio
async def my_func():
    asyncio.create_subprocess_exec('echo hello')
compliant.description: Async function with asyncio.create_subprocess_exec, which is an async OS call.

why_is_this_an_issue: Synchronous OS calls in async functions can lead to performance issues and potential deadlocks.
how_to_fix: Use async OS calls or move synchronous calls outside of async functions.

checks.message: Async function contains synchronous OS call.
